original: Publication of "The Fellowship of the Ring," the first volume of "TheLord of the Rings" byJ. R. R. Tolkien, by George Allen & Unwin in London
Mariner 6 begins transmitting distant-encounter photos of Mars
Javier Sotomayor of Cuba sets a high jump record at 8.046 ft (2.45 m) in San Juan, Puerto Rico
Astronomers announce their discovery of dwarf planet Eris
Test footage is first created forWalt Disney's "Steamboat Willie," featuring Mickey Mouse
US PresidentEisenhowersigns into law the National Aeronautics and Space Act of 1958, establishing NASA
Ben & Jerry's Ice Cream andJerry Garciaagree on a new flavor: Cherry Garcia, featuring cherry ice cream with cherries and fudge flakes
Bob Dylanis hurt in a motorcycle accident near Woodstock, New York
American Penny Dean swims the English Channel in a record 7 hours and 40 minutes; record holds until 1995
Yevgeni Sadovyi of the Unified Team swims a world record in the 400 m freestyle in 3:45.00 to complete the 200-400 m freestyle double at the Barcelona Olympics
SirRobert Baden-Powellforms the Boy Scouts in England

George Dixon, Canadian boxer (World Bantamweight title, 1892; World Featherweight champion, 1891-97 & 1898-1901; 1st Black athlete to win a World C'ship in any sport), born in Halifax, Nova Scotia (d. 1908)
Major League record 26 strikeouts: Phillies (16), Pirates (10)
American Michael Ray Barrowman swims a world record in the 200 m breaststroke (2:10.16) at the World Championships in Perth, Australia, breaking his own record set in August 1991
The Roman Senate and Praetorian Guard proclaim 13-year-old Gordian III as Emperor - the youngest-ever emperor of a united Rome
Emperor Go-Horikawa, aged only 10 years old, ascends to the Chrysanthemum Throne of Japan
Ten Spanish treasure galleons sink off Florida's coast in a hurricane
Olympic National Park is established in Washington state, featuring the longest undeveloped coastline in America
Philadelphia Phillies lose 4-3 to the SF Giants at Connie Mack Stadium, the first of an MLB record 23 consecutive defeats
Jacques Cousteau's Calypso anchors in a record 7,500 meters of water
The "Invincible" Spanish Armada is sighted approaching England, and several skirmishes follow, forcing the invading fleet to make a long and costly retreat around Scotland and Ireland
Isabel, Princess Imperial of Brazil (while serving as regent signed law abolishing slavery), born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil (d. 1921)
Dovie Hudson, American civil rights activist in rural Mississippi, filed 1st lawsuit to desegregate public schools, born in Mississippi (d. 1994)
Nellie Kim, Soviet gymnast (Olympic-3 golds-1976), born in Shurab, Tajik SSR
First United States Congress elections in Hawaii as a state of the Union

Cochrane and Matheson forest fire, 500,000 acres (2,000 km²) in size in Ontario, takes approximately 223 lives and leads to improved techniques and legislation for the prevention and control of forest fires
MLB St. Louis Cardinals'Lou Brocksteals his 700th base
PirateHonus Wagner, at 41, hits a grand slam home run
Monica SelesdefeatsMartina Navratilovain her return to tennis after she was stabbed in an on-court attack in 1993
Bernhard Zondek, German-Israeli gynaecologist (developed 1st reliable pregnancy test), born in Wronke, Germany (d. 1966)
Ken Burns [Kenneth Lauren], American director and documentary film producer (The Civil War, Baseball), born in Brooklyn, New York

Danger Mouse [Brian Burton], American alternative rock and electronic hip-hop musician, songwriter and Grammy Award-winning record producer (The Grey Album; Gnarls Barkley; Black Keys), born in White Plains, New York
Dak Prescott, American football quarterback (NFL Offensive Rookie of the Year 2016; Walter Payton NFL Man of the Year 2022; Pro Bowl 2016, 18; Dallas Cowboys), born in Sulphur, Louisiana
Annibale de Gasparis discovers asteroid 15 Eunomia
First successful transcontinental US airmail flight from New York to San Francisco
Airship R100 makes the first passenger-carrying flight from England to Canada
First non-stop transpacific flight by a jet
A 6.5 earthquake strikes Caracas, Venezuela, causing significant damage
Temperature reaches 103°F (39.44°C) in Seattle, Washington, the hottest day on record
Cleveland's 125th anniversary celebration:Cy Young, aged 54, pitches two innings
Floyd PattersonTKO's Tommy Jackson in the 10th round for the heavyweight boxing title
First show by rock trio "Cream," consisting of guitaristEric Clapton, bassist Jack Bruce, and drummer Ginger Baker, at the Twisted Wheel nightclub in Manchester, England
"The Princess Diaries," starringAnne Hathaway(in her film debut), featuringJulie Andrews, and directed byGarry Marshall, premieres at El Capitan theater in Hollywood
Armauer Hansen, Norwegian physician (1st to identify leprosy as bacteria), born in Bergen, Norway (d. 1912)[1]
Jim Marshall, British businessman and electric guitar amplifier pioneer known as "The Father of Loud", born in Acton, West London, England (d. 2012)
"Captain" Lou Albano, American professional wrestler, 1953-69, wrestler manager (1969-95), and actor (Cyndi Lauper music videos; Super Mario Brothers), born in Rome, Kingdom of Italy (d. 2009)
Michael S. Turner, American theoretical cosmologist, who coined the term dark energy, born in Los Angeles, California
115°F (46°C) is recorded in Holly Springs, Mississippi (state record)
Beatles movie "Help!" has Royal World Premiere at the London Pavilion Theatre in the West End of London; Princess Margaret and the Earl of Snowdon attend
David Bowiestars in the dramatic stage play "The Elephant Man" at the Auditorium Theatre in Denver, Colorado

Tina Turnerfilms the music video for the song “One Of The Living” at Eastern State Penitentiary in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; the song is from the film "Mad Max: Beyond Thunderdome," in which she co-stars with Mel Gibson
Isidor Isaac Rabi, American nuclear physicist who won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1944 for his discovery of nuclear magnetic resonance, born in Rymanów, Galicia, Austria-Hungary (d. 1988)
Dave Stevens, American illustrator and comic artist (The Rocketeer), born in Lynwood, California (d. 2008)
Richard Steven Horvitz, American comic voice actor, born in Los Angeles, California
Wil Wheaton, American actor (Star Trek Next Generation-Wesley, Stand By Me), born in Burbank, California
Ashley McBryde, American country music singer-songwriter ("Girl Goin' Nowhere"; "A Little Dive Bar in Dahlonega"), born in Waldron, Arkansas
The Doors' single "Light My Fire" hits #1 in the US
Authorities reverse ban on steel-string guitars in prison cells in England and Wales after a campaign by rock guitarists including Billy Bragg,David Gilmour, and Johnny Marr
John Graves Simcoe decides to build a fort and settlement at Toronto after sailing into the bay
Treaty of Amity and Commerce, also known as the Harris Treaty, is signed between the US and Japan, opening Japanese ports to trade
